compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Review-result sidecars (plan- and code-review `REVIEW_RESULT` events) are pruned to the most
recent `RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 10` per ticket. Drift-refresh re-reviews on a single ticket can
exceed 10 rounds, so an auditor loses the earlier rounds. Raise the retention bound to 50 — well
above the observed single-digit remediation-loop depth — while still capping unbounded growth of
`REVIEW_RESULT` events on the `tickets` branch (the hot path of every write). It is a deliberate
bound, not unlimited retention.

## Current state (the plan review's correction)

`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` HAS a `prune()` function and a `RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 10`
constant, and its `emit()` calls `prune()`. **`src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` has NEITHER
a `prune()` function NOR a `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` constant, and its `emit()` does not prune** —
code-review `REVIEW_RESULT` events currently grow unbounded. So this story does two things: it
RAISES the plan-review bound to 50, AND it ADDS an equivalent prune path to code-review governed
by the SAME shared constant, so both paths retain identically.

## Scope

- `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py` — raise `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` from `10` to `50`. Keep the
  prune newest-first (ordering unchanged); only the retained count changes.
- `src/rebar/llm/code_review/sidecar.py` — ADD retention governed by the SAME constant:
  - reference the shared bound (import `RETAIN_PER_TICKET` from `rebar.llm.plan_review.sidecar`,
    so there is ONE named constant governing both paths — no second literal to drift);
  - add a `prune(ticket_id, *, keep=RETAIN_PER_TICKET, repo_root=None)` function mirroring
    `plan_review.sidecar.prune` (list the ticket's `-REVIEW_RESULT.json` events by filename
    timestamp, `git rm` all but the newest `keep`, commit with `--no-verify`; best-effort and
    exception-swallowing — a failed prune never fails the gate);
  - call `prune(target_ticket, repo_root=repo_root)` from `emit()` after `append_event`, matching
    plan-review's emit → prune sequence.

## Approach rationale

A single shared constant (imported, not duplicated) keeps the change minimal and makes "governs
both paths" literally true — no new config key (YAGNI: no consumer has asked for a per-repo
override; a config key can be added later if a real need appears). The code-review prune reuses
the exact newest-first-by-filename-timestamp strategy plan-review already uses, so both paths
behave identically.

## Compatibility & rollback

Purely a bound increase plus a new (previously absent) code-review prune. No schema/format change.
Already-pruned plan-review history is not recovered (already deleted); going forward more is
retained. Code-review sidecars that already grew unbounded will be pruned to 50 on the next emit.
Reverting sets the constant back to 10 (prunes more aggressively) and/or removes the code-review
prune (code-review growth becomes unbounded again); no migration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] After 50+ reviews on one ticket, up to 50 `REVIEW_RESULT` sidecars are retained and a write
      past 50 prunes the oldest — for BOTH the plan-review and the code-review sidecar. Oracle: a
      unit test appending N>50 synthetic events on each path (via each module's `prune`) and
      asserting the retained count is exactly 50 and the newest are the ones kept.
- [ ] The retention cap is a SINGLE definition, not duplicated: `plan_review.sidecar` defines
      `RETAIN_PER_TICKET = 50` and `code_review.sidecar` obtains the SAME value by importing it
      (no second `= <literal>` in code_review). Oracle: a test asserting
      `code_review.sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET == plan_review.sidecar.RETAIN_PER_TICKET == 50`, and
      that each module's `prune` defaults its `keep` to that constant.
- [ ] `code_review.sidecar.emit` prunes after appending (the previously-absent path now exists).
      Oracle: a test that emitting more than the cap on a code-review target ticket leaves exactly
      50 events.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
compiled_state.title: raise review-sidecar retention cap (10 -> 50, shared constant)
